items.title: 'I won't be a yes man': Co-chair of ACT Reconciliation council resigns

children.content: ACT Reconciliation Council co-chair Selina Walker resigns, saying she has lost faith in the territory government's approach to Indigenous affairs.

image.altText: A woman wearing a pink jumper.

items.title: Canberra's Jason Geria and Kai Trewin named in Socceroos' World Cup squad

children.content: Socceroos' coach Tony Popovic has 17 potential World Cup debutants in his team.

image.altText: Tony Popovic addresses the Socceroos squad during a training session

items.title: A planning rule wouldn't bend so the lights at this Canberra oval had to

children.content: If the six light poles skirting Manuka Oval look like they're on a lean, it's because they are. The 47-metre-high poles slant inwards because otherwise they couldn't be approved under an unusual Canberra planning rule.

gallery.altText: Very large curved floodlights stand above a major sportsground.

thumbnail.altText: Very large curved floodlights stand above a major sportsground.

items.title: Smoke engulfed their cities. Did it make their children sick?

children.content: Years after bushfires covered their cities in smoke, mothers are left wondering if their children's chronic illnesses began with what was in the air before they were born.

image.altText: Illustration of an ultrasound image of a fetus before a window with bushfires burning in the background.

items.title: Aboriginal ownership of national parks celebrated at cultural festival

children.content: The Yuin people on the NSW South Coast celebrate two decades of Aboriginal ownership of two national parks.

image.altText: a man leans down over smoke

items.title: ACT government's coercive control legislation won't lead to any charges for years

children.content: The government's bill does not detail specific coercive control behaviours which it says is deliberate.

gallery.altText: A woman speaks at a press conference.

thumbnail.altText: A woman speaks at a press conference.

items.title: Woman charged for trying to print child abuse material at Canberra store

children.content: A NSW woman is arrested in Canberra after staff at a photo printing business allegedly discovered child abuse material she tried and failed to print due to a machine malfunction.

image.altText: Badge on the sleeve of Australian Federal Police officer's uniform.

items.title: Canberra man jailed over sexual abuse of 'extremely vulnerable' child

children.content: A man who sexually assaulted a 12-year-old girl after a "chance encounter" at a Canberra shopping centre has been jailed, with the judge finding he took advantage of an "extremely vulnerable" child.

image.altText: ACT Magistrate Court

items.title: Spotlight on Welcome to Country ceremonies in wake of Anzac Day controversy

children.content: Indigenous elders explain why they believe Welcome to Country ceremonies have faced opposition, and why abandoning the practice would create a cultural "abyss".

gallery.altText: An Indigenous man in traditional cultural dress at a smoking ceremony
An Indigenous person stands next to the Balbuk Yooreel statue in a park
Noongar elder Aunty Tina Hayden carries out a Welcome to Country

thumbnail.altText: An Indigenous man in traditional cultural dress at a smoking ceremony
An Indigenous person stands next to the Balbuk Yooreel statue in a park

items.title: Eerie footage and DNA: What we know four months since Nancy Guthrie vanished

children.content: It has been four months since Nancy Guthrie, the mother of America's Today show co-host Savannah Guthrie, was reported missing. This is what we know about the alleged abduction.

image.altText: A photo from instagram of savannah guthrie and her mum at a sports game

items.title: Beneath the rubble of Liddell Power Station towers lay 'perfect' plan

children.content: Half a tonne of explosives was needed to bring the Hunter Valley's Liddell Power Station chimneys crashing down.

image.altText: Two concrete power station chimneys fall, one is in half and dust is spilling from the bases.

items.title: Police unlawfully arrested this man, broke his ribs, then charged him with assault

children.content: After fracturing about a dozen of a man's ribs and puncturing his lung, police officers discussed how to explain what happened to him.

gallery.altText: A man wearing a white polo with blue stripes is pushed into a holding cell by a police officer.
A man sits outdoors near a park, he looks ahead with a neutral expression. He is wearing a cap and a football jersey.

thumbnail.altText: A man wearing a white polo with blue stripes is pushed into a holding cell by a police officer.
A man sits outdoors near a park, he looks ahead with a neutral expression. He is wearing a cap and a football jersey.

items.title: Government loses bid to keep terror funding documents secret

children.content: Commissioner Virginia Bell says the documents will provide a comparison of the resourcing given to counterterrorism agencies before and after August 2024, when the terror threat level was raised to "probable".

image.altText: A woman looking intensely serious.

items.title: Sydney and Melbourne property values drop as housing market stalls in May

children.content: Australia's housing market continues to lose momentum, with national home values flatlining in May as higher interest rates, weak confidence and proposed property tax changes weigh on demand.

image.altText: Houses in Brisbane street

items.title: Strongest storm system in 49 years tears through Perth, southern WA

children.content: Tens of thousands of homes are without power and the SES receives hundreds of calls for help, with the roofs torn off two apartment buildings, after WA's southern half is hammered by more severe weather.

articles.title: South Australia braces for severe weather and damaging winds

children.content: The State Emergency Service has advised people to move vehicles away from trees and secure loose items around properties as South Australia braces for damaging winds.

image.altText: Two women cross the street in the rain with one protected under an umbrella and the other pulling it towards her.

gallery.altText: Four SES workers in orange hi-vis clothing work to repair the damaged roof of a house with a fallen tree lying on it.
A badly damaged apartment block with its roof torn off and metal debris.
A side fence lies on the ground next to a pool in the backyard of a house after being blown over in a storm.

items.title: ASX 200 finishes flat after clawing back early losses, oil higher — as it happened

children.content: The ASX 200 has finished largely flat after clawing back early losses, while oil prices rose as investors continued to monitor tensions in the Middle East. Look back on our business blog.

image.altText: Pedestrians are seen reflected on the Australian Stock Exchange trading board in Sydney

items.title: Search for man missing in East Arnhem Land ends after nine days

children.content: After a nine-day operation covering more than 1,300 square kilometres of land, the search for a 31-year-old man in East Arnhem Land has been stood down.

image.altText: A Northern Territory police vehicle in Katherine.

items.title: Four easy, tasty curries for busy winter weeknights

children.content: Curries are perfect for cooler months, and many come with a serving of immune-boosting properties.

children.content: Binding financial agreements take time and money to obtain. Here's how they work.

items.title: Roadside stalls are booming, with 45,000 members in one group alone

children.content: Nestled away in the Adelaide Hills are dozens of roadside stalls dedicated to local produce, including baked goods and bouquets, and business is booming.

image.altText: A woman with stands next to a wooden stall stocked with loaves of bread. She wears jeans and a tshirt, smiling

items.title: Broome local cringes as AI sells WA's north 'as something it's not'

children.content: A wave of AI-generated videos posted to social media causes a stir among tour operators in WA's north. They say the content does not accurately portray the region and threatens the preservation of Indigenous culture.

gallery.altText: Bright blue water with rocks in the foreground.
This AI-generated video claims to show the Kimberley's Devonian limestone reef, with "sunbathing crocodiles".

thumbnail.altText: Bright blue water with rocks in the foreground.
This AI-generated video claims to show the Kimberley's Devonian limestone reef, with "sunbathing crocodiles".

items.title: Why is Labor spending its political majority on this tax reform?

children.content: Why has Labor decided to spend the political capital of a large majority on a minor change to capital gains tax on businesses?

image.altText: Jim Chalmers and Anthony Albanese stand next to each other; both wear dark suit jackets.

items.title: Three-year-old child dies in Werribee house fire

children.content: A child has died in a house fire in Werribee and another person has been taken to hospital with serious injuries.

items.title: Chadstone cold case accused faces court after 30 years

children.content: A 78-year-old man accused of shooting three people in an Armaguard robbery at a busy cinema in 1994 faces court.

image.altText: Police car next to Armaguard vehicle in Chadstone shopping centre car park.

items.title: 'Loving' parents not at fault for deaths of children left in hot cars, coroner finds

children.content: The deaths of two children who were left in cars during hot weather were the result of their parents' memory failure, a NSW coroner finds.

image.altText: Child's arm dangles while in a car seat.

items.title: Decades-old trees in Darwin finally flower for the first and only time

children.content: About 20 Gebang palms have flowered in Darwin in a rare spectacle, reaffirming the local council's plans to foster a new generation of the trees.

gallery.altText: A close-up shot of a Gebang palm tree in flower, with small yellow tufts growing outward.
A man wearing a hat and holding a camera, looking into the distance as he stands underneath a flowering palm tree.
A group of tall palm trees beside a lake.

items.title: Shein, Kmart fight back against Qld retailer in intellectual property dispute

children.content: Shein and Kmart have fired back at Sabo Skirt in an intellectual property dispute, saying the designs have been accused of copying are substantially similar to pre-existing designs.

items.title: Adelaide Hills man pleads guilty to murdering mother

children.content: A man who previously denied murdering his mother at their home in 2022, and even gave evidence in his own defence, has a last-minute change of heart and pleads guilty to murder.

image.altText: A bald man with a beard wearing handcuffs and a plaid shirt

items.title: Paddle-steamer first launched 120 years ago returns to working life

children.content: After being raised from the bottom of the river, dug out of the mud, and lovingly restored, the PS Canally is ready to take passengers up and down the River Murray.

image.altText: The PS Canally is moored on a river. It is white and grey with colourful flags hung up on it.

items.title: Thousands of green sea turtle hatchlings swim to sea in conservation win

children.content: The project at Raine Island, where 90 per cent of northern green sea turtles nest, has been working for a decade to improve hatching success.

A hand holding two round white eggs while another hand reaches under a turtle shell.

items.title: Italian and Brazilian authorities investigate possible Ebola cases as outbreak grows

children.content: The true reach of the Ebola outbreak in central Africa could be much wider than the official figures published by central African authorities, the World Health Organization is warning.

image.altText: A person wearing full-body and face PPE including a face mask standing in a medical tent.

items.title: Australia could still buy five US submarines if needed, defence minister says

children.content: Defence Minister Richard Marles has defended a shake-up to AUKUS that would involve Australia buying three second-hand Virginia-class submarines from the United States.

image.altText: Richard Marles speaks during an AUKUS Defence ministers' press huddle

items.title: Why Naomi Osaka and Taylor Townsend's dinner party 'caused a stir'

children.content: From the Harlem Renaissance of the 20s and 30s to Soul Food Sundays at Wimbledon in the 80s and 90s, Naomi Osaka and Taylor Townsend's dinner party before the French Open continues a long history of black athletes coming together.

gallery.altText: Coco Gauff, Gael Monfils, Naomi Osaka, Taylor Townsend, Asia Muhammad and Christopher Eubanks stand arm in arm.
Tennis players Naomi Osaka and Taylor Townsend laugh together.

thumbnail.altText: Coco Gauff, Gael Monfils, Naomi Osaka, Taylor Townsend, Asia Muhammad and Christopher Eubanks stand arm in arm.
Tennis players Naomi Osaka and Taylor Townsend laugh together.
